title: Dual-assertion skill protocol test: user language vs artifact language
context: When a skill operates bilingually (Spanish user chat + English YAML artifacts), a single test per concern is insufficient. FEAT-006 introduced a protocol test that independently asserts both invariants to prevent regression on either axis.
content: For any Quorum skill that produces machine-readable artifacts while communicating with the user in a different language, write a protocol test with two independent assertions: (1) assert the user-facing output language (e.g., check for Spanish keywords or ESPERANDO RESPUESTA DEL USUARIO... marker), and (2) assert the artifact content language separately (e.g., scan generated YAML fields for absence of Spanish-only characters or presence of required English keywords). Keep these as distinct test cases in tests/test_skill_protocol.py — one failure must not mask the other. Name them with the pattern test_<skill>_<concern> (e.g., test_q_brief_communication_protocol_is_spanish, test_q_brief_generated_spec_content_is_english).
anti_patterns: Writing a single combined test that checks user language and artifact language together — a regression in one concern can pass if the other assertion still holds.
